or *cachanilla* (Mexico), is a [rhizomatous](/wiki/Rhizomatous "Rhizomatous") [evergreen](/wiki/Evergreen "Evergreen") [shrub](/wiki/Shrub "Shrub") of riparian areas in the lower [Sonoran Desert](/wiki/Sonoran_Desert "Sonoran Desert") and surrounding areas. It is common in the lower [Colorado River](/wiki/Colorado_River "Colorado River") valley of [California](/wiki/California "California"), [Nevada](/wiki/Nevada "Nevada") and [Arizona](/wiki/Arizona "Arizona"), as far east as [Texas](/wiki/Texas "Texas"), and in northern [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") where it often forms dense impenetrable thickets. It is a perennial shrub and grows along watercourses.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
["Lady Bird Johnson Wildflower Center - The University of Texas at Austin"](https://www.wildflower.org/plants/result.php?id_plant=PLSE). *www.wildflower.org*. Retrieved 2022-05-18.

Seattle, Washington: University of Washington Press, on behalf of the American Ethnological Society. p. 81. From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Arrowweed](/w/index.php?title=Arrowweed&redirect=no "Arrowweed"))

Species of plant

| *Pluchea sericea* | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| [Scientific classification](/wiki/Taxonomy_(biology) "Taxonomy (biology)") [Edit this classification](/wiki/Template:Taxonomy/Pluchea "Edit this classification") | |
| Kingdom: | [Plantae](/wiki/Plant "Plant") |
| *Clade*: | [Tracheophytes](/wiki/Vascular_plant "Vascular plant") |
| *Clade*: | [Angiosperms](/wiki/Flowering_plant "Flowering plant") |
| *Clade*: | [Eudicots](/wiki/Eudicots "Eudicots") |
| *Clade*: | [Asterids](/wiki/Asterids "Asterids") |
| Order: | [Asterales](/wiki/Asterales "Asterales") |
| Family: | [Asteraceae](/wiki/Asteraceae "Asteraceae") |
| Genus: | [*Pluchea*](/wiki/Pluchea "Pluchea") |
| Species: | ***P. sericea*** |
| [Binomial name](/wiki/Binomial_nomenclature "Binomial nomenclature") | |
| ***Pluchea sericea***  [Nutt.](/wiki/Nutt. "Nutt.") | |

***Pluchea sericea***, commonly called **arrowweed** or *cachanilla* (Mexico), is a [rhizomatous](/wiki/Rhizomatous "Rhizomatous") [evergreen](/wiki/Evergreen "Evergreen") [shrub](/wiki/Shrub "Shrub") of riparian areas in the lower [Sonoran Desert](/wiki/Sonoran_Desert "Sonoran Desert") and surrounding areas. It is common in the lower [Colorado River](/wiki/Colorado_River "Colorado River") valley of [California](/wiki/California "California"), [Nevada](/wiki/Nevada "Nevada") and [Arizona](/wiki/Arizona "Arizona"), as far east as [Texas](/wiki/Texas "Texas"), and in northern [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") where it often forms dense impenetrable thickets. It is a perennial shrub and grows along watercourses.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Uses

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pluchea_sericea&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Uses")]

It was once used medicinally by [Native Americans](/wiki/Indigenous_peoples_of_the_Americas "Indigenous peoples of the Americas") as an [antidiarrheal](/wiki/Antidiarrhoeal "Antidiarrhoeal") and [eyewash](/wiki/Eyewash "Eyewash"). Other traditional uses include [thatching](/wiki/Thatching "Thatching"), [arrowmaking](/wiki/Arrow "Arrow") and food, especially the edible [root](/wiki/Root "Root").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In other uses, the gum resin that exudes from the plant was used by the [Papago Indians](/wiki/Papago_Indians "Papago Indians") to make a mending glue on broken pottery.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pluchea_sericea&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: References")]

2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** ["BRIT - Native American Ethnobotany Database"](http://naeb.brit.org/redirect/?referer=). *naeb.brit.org*.
3. **[^](#cite_ref-3)** Fontana, Bernard L.; Robinson, William J.; Cormack, Charles W.; Leavitt, Earnest E. (1962). *Papago Indian Pottery*. Seattle, Washington: University of Washington Press, on behalf of the American Ethnological Society. p. 81. [OCLC](/wiki/OCLC_(identifier) "OCLC (identifier)") [869680](https://search.worldcat.org/oclc/869680).
